sn7.14:1.2: Then a certain very rich brahman—shabby, shabbily dressed—went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, exchanged courteous greetings with him. After this exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side.
sn7.14:1.3: As he was sitting there the Blessed One said to him,
sn7.14:1.4: “Why, brahman, are you shabby & shabbily dressed?”
sn7.14:1.5: “Just now, Master Gotama, my four sons—at their wives’
sn7.14:1.6: instigation—threw me out of the house.”
sn7.14:1.7: “In that case, brahman, memorize these verses and then recite them when a large assembly of people have gathered in the town hall and your sons are sitting there, too.
sn7.14:2.4: at their wives’ instigation have chased me away, as dogs would swine.
sn7.14:3.3: demons in the disguise of sons
sn7.14:3.4: who abandon me in old age.
sn7.14:4.4: so the elderly father of those foolish boys begs at other people’s homes.
sn7.14:6.1: In the dark it goes before me;
sn7.14:6.2: down steep slopes, it gives support.
sn7.14:6.3: Through the power of my staff,
sn7.14:6.4: when I stumble I still stand firm.”
sn7.14:7.1: Then the very rich brahman, having memorized these verses in the presence of the Blessed One, recited them when a large assembly of people had gathered in the town hall and his sons were sitting there, too:
sn7.14:8.4: at their wives’ instigation have chased me away, as dogs would swine.
sn7.14:9.3: demons in the disguise of sons
sn7.14:9.4: who abandon me in old age.
sn7.14:10.4: so the elderly father of those foolish boys begs at other people’s homes.
sn7.14:12.1: In the dark it goes before me;
sn7.14:12.2: down steep slopes, it gives support.
sn7.14:12.3: Through the power of my staff,
sn7.14:12.4: when I stumble I still stand firm.”
sn7.14:13.1: Then the brahman’s sons, having led him home, bathed him, and each provided him in a pair of cloths.
sn7.14:13.2: So the brahman, taking one pair of cloths, went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, exchanged courteous greetings with him.
sn7.14:13.3: After this exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side. As he was sitting there he said to the Blessed One,
sn7.14:13.4: “We brahmans, Master Gotama, look for a teacher’s fee for our teacher.
sn7.14:13.5: May Master Gotama accept this teacher’s portion from me.”
sn7.14:13.6: The Blessed One accepted it out of sympathy.
sn7.14:13.7: Then the very rich brahman said to the Blessed One:
sn7.14:13.8: “Magnificent, Master Gotama! Magnificent! Just as if he were to place upright what was overturned, to reveal what was hidden, to show the way to one who was lost, or to carry a lamp into the dark so that those with eyes could see forms, in the same way has Master Gotama—through many lines of reasoning—made the Dhamma clear. I go to Master Gotama for refuge, to the Dhamma, & to the Saṅgha of monks.
sn7.14:13.9: May Master Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge from this day forward, for life.”
